perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the meanings ascribed thereto in this Section 1: "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Person, any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such Person. For purposes of this definition, the term "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") means the direct or indirect power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the meanings ascribed thereto in this Section 1: "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Person, any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such Person. For purposes of this definition, the term "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") means the direct or indirect power to influence, but not necessarily direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person. Control can be established through informal agreements or relationships, without requiring ownership of voting securities or a formal contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflicting definition of 'control' within the definition of 'Affiliate.' Originally, 'control' required the power to direct the management and policies of a person. The modified definition broadens this to include the power to influence without direct authority, creating an in-text contradiction. This impacts interpretations throughout the document where 'Affiliate' status is relevant, particularly concerning responsibility for breaches and exercise of rights under the agreement. Allscripts' Affiliates may exercise Allscripts' rights and fulfill its related obligations under this Agreement, provided that Allscripts shall be responsible for any breach of such obligations by its Affiliates to the same extent as if Allscripts was the breaching party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Allscripts' Affiliates may exercise Allscripts' rights and fulfill its related obligations under this Agreement, provided that Allscripts shall be responsible for any breach of such obligations by its Affiliates to the same extent as if Allscripts was the breaching party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'control' to include 'influence' without direct authority, whereas the original text required the 'power to direct' management and policies. This contradicts the clause stating Allscripts is responsible for breaches by its Affiliates, because with the new definition, 'Affiliates' may now include entities with mere 'influence,' creating uncertainty about which entities Allscripts is responsible for. The changed definition creates ambiguity concerning the scope of Allscripts' responsibility for the actions of its Affiliates, and is a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: "Sublicensed Customer" means an Allscripts Customer that has purchased a Subscription from Allscripts or its Affiliates or that has entered into a Merchant Agreement with Company (except in connection with a sale of PIMS directly or indirectly by Company).
perturbation.changed_text: "Sublicensed Customer" means an Allscripts Customer that has purchased a Subscription from Allscripts, even if such a Subscription is purchased through unauthorized channels or without a formal agreement, provided such customers are using the services. For avoidance of doubt, the term also applies to any customer using any trial version of the software.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Sublicensed Customer' requires a purchase of a subscription from authorized sources. The modified definition broadens this to include customers using the services without a formal agreement or through unauthorized channels, including trial versions. This introduces a contradiction because the agreement outlines specific terms and conditions governing Sublicensed Customers, which become unclear if unauthorized users are also considered Sublicensed Customers. Merchant Processing Services may be provided by Company in conjunction with the Subscription Software Services but are not, for purposes of this Agreement, deemed Subscription Software Services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, the terms and conditions relating to the provision of Merchant Processing Services to Sublicensed Customers, including but not limited to commencement thereof, shall be set forth in the Merchant Application and Merchant Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Sublicensed Customer' as someone who purchased a subscription from authorized sources. The changed text broadens this definition to include users without formal agreements or through unauthorized channels, even trial users. The contradicted text states that 'Merchant Processing Services to Sublicensed Customers...shall be set forth in the Merchant Application and Merchant Agreement.' If a 'Sublicensed Customer' now includes trial users or unauthorized users, it's unclear whether these users are also subject to the terms of the Merchant Application and Merchant Agreement, thus creating a direct contradiction and ambiguity in the agreement's applicability and enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: Updates may be customer facing (i.e. updates that are directly displayed to the customer such as new features, etc.) or non- customer facing (such as bug fixes or workarounds that are not directly displayed to the customer).
perturbation.changed_text: Updates are defined strictly as new customer facing features and enhancements only, and exclude bug fixes, workarounds, or any other non-customer facing changes. Such non-customer facing alterations shall be termed 'Maintenance Patches' and are governed under a separate schedule outlined in Exhibit M.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition included both customer-facing and non-customer-facing changes as 'Updates.' The modified definition restricts 'Updates' to only customer-facing features, creating a new category called 'Maintenance Patches.' This contradicts the sections that refer to updates including bug fixes or other non-customer facing alterations. For example, in 13.1 says, such Updates are released or generally made available [***]. For the avoidance of doubt, Updates will constitute Installed Software or Subscription Software Services (as applicable) and be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the avoidance of doubt, Updates will constitute Installed Software or Subscription Software Services (as applicable) and be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. With respect to the Merchant Processing, [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Updates" to exclude bug fixes and non-customer facing alterations, categorizing these as "Maintenance Patches." This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that "Updates will constitute Installed Software or Subscription Software Services...and be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement." Since the definition of 'Updates' has been narrowed, the contradicted text now includes a term that no longer means what it originally did, leading to a direct conflict. The contradiction falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, due to the inconsistent definition of 'Updates' within the document.
